title: Transactional writes are idempotent and fully audited
statement: Transactional writes (RENACH/RENAINF POST/PATCH) are idempotent when the client supplies an Idempotency-Key header: a replay with the same key returns the original {status, body} without creating a duplicate. Without an Idempotency-Key the write runs its full logic, so duplicate/already-done business rules fire on a plain replay (RENAINF.AIT.DUPLICATED, RENAINF.PENALTY.ALREADY_IMPOSED, RENAINF.PAYMENT.ALREADY_SETTLED → 402). Every state transition writes an audit.evento row with the payload and a SHA-256 payload hash, recording situacao_anterior/situacao_nova and the cpf_usuario. No state changes without a corresponding audit record.
rationale: The reference architecture mandates idempotency and full auditability for transactional operations; retries are normal for real clients and must not double-apply penalties, exams, or payments.
